message:
fuzz(traversal_dominance): grow seed corpus with 266 new inputs

Extend the fuzz_traversal_dominance corpus that was introduced in
8b586c3a with an additional 266 seed inputs harvested by libFuzzer
during local fuzzing runs. Seeds are content-addressed (hex SHA-1
filenames) so duplicates are naturally deduplicated and the corpus
stays append-only.

The traversal/dominance target exercises BFS, DFS, dominator-tree,
and articulation-point algorithms from fnx-algorithms, and the new
seeds extend coverage of:

- mixed-direction graphs (directed + undirected components)
- self-loops and parallel edges (MultiGraph paths)
- pathological articulation/bridge configurations
- larger node-count graphs that previously timed out
- attribute-bearing nodes/edges that round-trip through the
  traversal invariants

These should reduce future fuzzing time-to-coverage and let the
nightly fuzzing job spend its compute budget on novel mutations
instead of rediscovering already-covered states.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
